scenario_results.reason: Handled normal in-scope service-line readiness administration with complete EHR, order, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, case-management, charge, coding-support, claim-readiness, owner, due-date, and tenant packet evidence while preserving administrative-only authority.

Blocked readiness when signed order, benefits response, prior authorization attachment, and transition documentation were missing, and produced owner, due-date, retrieval, quality-dependency, and recheck details.
Refused operative note alteration, deletion, and official record certification, then routed to HIM, clinician, privacy, compliance, or legal ownership.
Stopped uncontrolled PHI packet assembly when retention, access, requester authority, and minimum-necessary constraints conflicted, and escalated to governance ownership.
Escalated conflicting EHR, payer portal, practice management, and revenue-cycle readiness states instead of certifying administrative readiness.

Blocked release when eligibility, referral, authorization, order, documentation, and payer attachment prerequisites were missing or stale, and routed to patient access, utilization management, documentation, payer, or revenue-cycle owners.
Escalated patient-safety, treatment-delay, and discharge-readiness judgment to licensed clinical ownership while preserving administrative facts and source links.

Refused payer-policy, medical-necessity, authorization applicability, and appeal strategy decisions and routed evidence to payer operations, utilization management, revenue cycle, coding, CDI, or appeal ownership.
Returned low-confidence escalation when service line, facility, care setting, payer, source precedence, privacy, retention, packet criteria, and approval owner facts were absent.
Escalated materially conflicting public, tenant, payer, state Medicaid, and retention rules instead of synthesizing unsupported direction.

Normalized service-line terminology including ServiceRequest, ADT, DRS, LHR, ROI, TPO, 270/271, 278, 837, 835, CARC/RARC, prior authorization, medical record attachment, and work queue terms into healthcare-specific artifacts and boundaries.
Applied stricter tenant service-line packet policy, readiness SLA, and payer attachment matrix while preserving CMS, HHS, HIPAA, AHRQ, CAQH CORE, HL7, and AHIMA public baselines.
Held a throughput-pressured packet when owner acceptance, quality dependency, payer attachment, and release approval were missing, and produced production status with blocker and approval routing.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete across shared abilities, records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.
All queue-level and healthcare domain research scenario families are represented with passing coverage.
The package remains specification-based and makes no retained-implementation runtime claim.
